description: A viral infection of your nose and throat (upper respiratory tract). It's usually harmless, although it might not feel that way.
causes: Many types of viruses can cause a common cold. Rhinoviruses are the most common cause.
treatments: There's no cure for the common cold. Antibiotics aren't useful against cold viruses. Treatment is directed at relieving signs and symptoms.
precautions: Wash your hands. Disinfect your stuff. Use tissues. Don't share.

description: A common viral infection that can be deadly, especially in high-risk groups.
causes: Influenza is caused by viruses that infect the nose, throat, and lungs.
treatments: Flu is primarily treated with rest and fluid to allow the body to fight the infection on its own. Over-the-counter pain relievers may help with symptoms.
precautions: Get a yearly flu vaccine. Wash hands frequently. Avoid close contact with sick people.

description: A group of diseases that result in too much sugar in the blood (high blood glucose).
causes: The exact cause of most types of diabetes is unknown. In all cases, sugar builds up in the bloodstream.
treatments: Treatment includes diet, exercise, medication, and insulin therapy.
precautions: Maintain a healthy weight. Eat healthy foods. Get more physical activity.

description: A condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high.
causes: High blood pressure usually develops over time. It can happen because of unhealthy lifestyle choices.
treatments: Eating a healthier diet with less salt, exercising regularly, and taking medications can help lower blood pressure.
precautions: Reduce salt intake. Exercise regularly. Maintain a healthy weight. Limit alcohol.
